question_text: The Moon takes approximately 28 days to orbit Earth. During this time, we see the Moon's shape change from a thin crescent to a full circle and back again. What is the name of this changing pattern of light and dark on the Moon?
explanation: The Moon doesn't produce its own light; it reflects sunlight. As the Moon orbits Earth, different amounts of the sunlit side face us, creating the phases we observe over the 28-day cycle.
